title: rubus × mortuorum
intro: Rubus × mortuorum, a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus, is a type of blackberry plant known for its dark, edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant contains anthocyanins and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Consuming Rubus × mortuorum fruits or using them in herbal preparations may provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as support for cardiovascular and digestive health. Rubus × mortuorum is often used in herbal teas, jams, and tinctures, with the dried leaves and roots also being used in traditional medicine.
